item.title: I WANT TO BE A PART OF IT
item.content:encoded:
Brilliant, frustrating, haunting, difficult. I am incredibly happy to see this confounding masterwork back on the site, as it’s one of my favorites on the wiki. This article has stayed with me at night for four years now.

New York is dead, long live New York

+1 with extreme enthusiasm.
I just don't understand what's happening, or why any of this is necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so so glad to see this back

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember reading this years ago; specifically the imagery of Boeing and his plane crashing into New York in a nuclear explosion over and over again, and the comment from footnote 20. Although Alex's snarky comments felt out of place at times, I enjoyed this overall.

Glad to see this back on the site. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it but I feel like I’m missing something

How does the hand operative have the foundation report? Why did someone who (I assume) works for the foundation need to get this report from someone in the Hand?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This definitely feels like part of a canon that doesn't exist (yet?). My first read had me feeling an "oops, I clicked on entry #4 in a hub by accident and I don't get the references" sort of vibe. That said, I do like what's here, and I would love to have more context in the form of tales or accompanying objects, because the concept is hinting at interesting things and Alex seems like a fun narrator. Also, I was given the sense that *I* was missing a reference, not that the piece was just pulling from nothing, so there's definitely something that feels consistent even if the current version is somewhat baffling at first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I hadn't read it, I'd hate the idea of it, but Alex's charm won me over. I love it when an SCP article demonstrates that the Foundation members who wrote it have messed up in some way, and the reveal at the end was exactly vague enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I object less to the format screw and more to the weird lore of this article. I don't know these characters and I'm not ever given an opportunity to; what's up with one of them being a time traveler/body switcher at the end, or the Hand having "memetic conditioning"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, feels a bit too format screwy for me. -1
